questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: I. In a survey conducted recently in America, almost 70% of Americans said they find it easier to care for their cars than their personal health. 40% of them said they would be more likely to address their issues with their car than their health. II. Base on the above survey, the national campaign network for men's health launched this awareness through Abbott Laboratories to encourage men to visit their doctors more often to enjoy better health.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: I. The local cooperative credit society has decided to stop giving loans to farmers with immediate effect. II. A large number of credit society members have withdrawn major parts of their deposits from the credit society.

solutions.solution: (b) Since, Ahmed's mother take care of what he eats, Ahmed has a good health.
(b) Ravi died because of the accident.
(d) The two statements seem to have contrary causes.
(b) The fighting has led to the closure of the shops.
(e) It seems the price of Petroleum has increased in general. Alternatively, subsidies may have been reduced, again a cause common to both the statements.

(e) It can be safely assumed that party 'X' was earlier in the opposition and has benefitted from anti-incumbancy. But A and B are consequences of a common cause, i.e., bad governance by the ruling party.
(d) Since, the staff members have gone on strike, the help of outsiders has been sought.
(d) A seems to have happened as X is a better college. B seems to be the result of the falling standard of teaching in colleges.
(d) A might have happened because harassment of woman is on the rise while B seems to be the result of a change in gender role perception.
(d) The robberies have led to a demand of improvement in security situation.
(d) Clearly, calling of the strike and going on strike are events that may not be backed by the same cause. Therefore, they must have been effects triggered by separate independent causes.
(c) Clearly, each statement is self-sufficient in itself and stands independent of the other.
(b) The bumper crop has led to the largesse shown by the Government.
(c) A happened so that students could see more of the world. B happened so that the school may attend to other important task.
(d) A has happened because of increased supply of fruits. B has happened because of decreased supply of foodgrains.
(e) A and B both seem to have a common cause, agitation on a large scale.
(a) The fear of rain has led to the tournament being called off.
(b) Clearly, damage to crops due to high temperature may have resulted in a short supply of vegetables and an increase in their prices.

(c) The increase in the fees of the private colleges and there being no increase in the same in Government colleges seem to be policy matters undertaken by the individual decision boards at the two levels.
(e) Both the statements A and B are clearly backed by a common cause, which is clearly an increase in the number of thefts in the locality.
(c) It appears some special cosmic phenomenon has led to A and B.
(d) A certainly cannot be the effect of B, if we believe in an ethical world. A is the effect of bad policing and B of good policing.
(c) The removal of the signal post has led to the accident.
(b) A has been done in response to B.
(c) It is obvious from given statements that both are independent causes of action.

(d) Many people are opting for change from private sector to public sector because worldwide recession has created uncertainty in the job market. It is considered that public sector jobs are more certain than the private sector jobs. So, Statements A is the cause while Statements B is the effect.

(b) A large number of employees could not report to the duty on time because police had laid down barricades on the road to drop the miscreants. So, Statement B is the cause while Statement A is its effect.
(d) Both Statements A and B are effects of independent causes.
(c) Clearly, A is the cause and B is the effect.

(b) Government has decided to procure foodgrain earlier than the stipulated data because farmers are planning to sell their crops to private traders. So, Statement B is the cause and Statement A is its effect.
(d) Both statements are effects of some common cause as these are measures taken by Government.
(d) Since, both Statements I and II are effects of some common cause i.e., bitumen causing cancer to humans.
(c) Here, Statement I is the cause and Statement II is its effect i.e., after the survey is conducted, then based on that survey, national campaign network for men's health launched its awareness.

(b) Here, Statement II is the cause and Statement I is its effect i.e., since a large number of members have withdrawn major amount of deposits from the credit society, so the credit society has decided to stop giving loans to farmers with immediate effect.
